Title: Evolution-guided multiomics provide insights into the strengthening of bioactive flavone biosynthesis in medicinal pummelo

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Upstream general phenylpropanoid entry step feeding flavone biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Deaminates L-phenylalanine to form trans-cinnamic acid, supplying phenylpropanoid precursors for flavone biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key upstream control gene in this study because it is directly repressed by CmtMYB108 and its expression correlates with flavone accumulation, but not explicitly established as rate-limiting.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher in pummelo than in Citrus-related genera/wild citrus species; downregulated during fruit flavedo development; significantly decreased in CmtMYB108-overexpressing sweet orange leaves.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Vitexin; apigenin 7-O-glucoside; apigenin di-O-hexosyl-O-rhamnoside; apigenin 7-O-neohesperidoside; flavones
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Fruit peel, especially flavedo and albedo at early developmental stages
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive flavones with anti-inflammatory, anticancer, and antioxidative activities; medicinal basis of Huajuhong
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit developmental stage; evolutionary divergence/origin process of pummelo
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher PAL expression is associated with increased flavone accumulation in pummelo; repression of PAL accompanies reduced flavone/flavanone levels in CmtMYB108-overexpressing plants.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Developmental transcript profiling; correlation analysis; qRT-PCR in transgenic sweet orange; dual-luciferase transactivation assay with PAL promoter
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PAL expression is negatively correlated with CmtMYB108 and positively associated with flavone accumulation. 2. PAL expression is higher in pummelo than in Citrus-related genera and wild citrus species. 3. CmtMYB108 represses PAL promoter activity in transient assays, linking PAL to strengthened flavone biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful upstream engineering target to enhance medicinal flavone content, but likely best manipulated indirectly or in combination with downstream flavone genes to avoid broad pleiotropic effects.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Lyase; aromatic amino acid ammonia-lyase family
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Two PAL identifiers appear in different contexts: Cmt048800 in correlation analysis and Cmt241630 for the cloned promoter in luciferase assay; the paper treats PAL as a target module rather than dissecting individual PAL paralog functions.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CsPAL promoter (~2 kb upstream region) used in dual-luciferase assay

Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Mid-to-late branch step leading directly to flavone scaffold formation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts flavanones into flavones, directly generating the flavone backbone in the pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key control step for flavone accumulation; directly targeted and repressed by CmtMYB108.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Citrus maxima; Citrus sinensis; Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated then downregulated during fruit flavedo development; higher in pummelo than in Citrus-related genera/wild citrus species; significantly decreased in CmtMYB108-overexpressing sweet orange leaves.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Vitexin; apigenin 7-O-glucoside; apigenin di-O-hexosyl-O-rhamnoside; apigenin 7-O-neohesperidoside; flavones
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Fruit peel, especially flavedo and albedo at early developmental stages
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive flavones with anti-inflammatory, anticancer, and antioxidative activities; medicinal basis of Huajuhong
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit developmental stage; evolutionary divergence/origin process of pummelo
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher FNS expression is associated with stronger flavone accumulation in pummelo, whereas repression of FNS by CmtMYB108 reduces flavone and some flavanone accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Developmental transcript profiling; correlation analysis; qRT-PCR in transgenic sweet orange; dual-luciferase assay; yeast one-hybrid assay; metabolite profiling of overexpression lines
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FNS expression correlates positively with flavone accumulation and is higher in pummelo than in Citrus-related genera/wild citrus species. 2. CmtMYB108 represses FNS expression in sweet orange overexpression lines. 3. CmtMYB108 directly binds the FNS promoter and inhibits promoter activity, establishing FNS as a direct causal target controlling flavone biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A strong engineering target to elevate medicinal flavones in citrus; release of CmtMYB108 repression or enhanced FNS expression may improve flavone-rich medicinal germplasm.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper establishes FNS as the direct transcriptional target of CmtMYB108 by both LUC and Y1H assays; FNS is the clearest pathway enzyme causally linked to flavone strengthening.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: FNS promoter (~2 kb upstream region) used in dual-luciferase assay

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Citrus maxima; Citrus sinensis; Nicotiana benthamiana
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Significantly lower in pummelo than in Citrus-related genera and wild citrus species; grouped in transcript cluster 5 and upregulated during fruit development.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly negatively correlated with flavone levels and with expression of PAL, CHS, FNS, and F3'H in HZY fruit flavedo; increased in overexpression lines.
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Represses FNS directly and represses PAL promoter activity
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Promoter structural variation during pummelo evolution, including 0.6–0.9 kb deletion containing MITE/MYC motif and ~30 bp insertion-associated motif differences
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit developmental stage; evolutionary origin/divergence of pummelo
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Vitexin; apigenin 7-O-glucoside; apigenin di-O-hexosyl-O-rhamnoside; apigenin 7-O-neohesperidoside; flavones; naringin
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Fruit peel, especially flavedo and albedo at early developmental stages
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Anti-inflammatory, anticancer, antioxidative bioactive metabolites underlying medicinal value of Huajuhong
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: CmtMYB108 acts as a negative regulator of flavone biosynthesis; overexpression decreases total flavonoids in N. benthamiana and decreases 15 flavones and 3 flavanones in sweet orange.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mGWAS; developmental metabolome-transcriptome correlation; transient overexpression in N. benthamiana; stable overexpression in sweet orange; metabolomics; qRT-PCR; dual-luciferase assay; yeast one-hybrid assay
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: A decisive negative regulator of flavone accumulation identified by multiomics and functional assays; lower expression in pummelo contributes to strengthened bioactive flavone biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. mGWAS co-mapped several flavones to a locus near CmtMYB108, nominating it as the candidate regulator. 2. CmtMYB108 expression is negatively correlated with flavone levels and with flavone-pathway genes. 3. Overexpression in N. benthamiana and sweet orange reduces flavonoid/flavone accumulation. 4. CmtMYB108 represses PAL and FNS promoters, and directly binds FNS promoter in Y1H. 5. Lower natural expression in pummelo is associated with promoter structural variation and higher flavone accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Excellent target for breeding or genome editing to increase medicinal flavone content by weakening its expression or promoter activity; caution is needed because strong overexpression causes dwarfing, abnormal growth, and small leaves.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression in sweet orange caused abnormal growth, dwarfing, and minimal leaves; likely reflects broad developmental roles and/or need for flavones in growth.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome assembly BioProject PRJNA911419; RNA-seq for gene annotation SRR22744601; metabolomics of 403 metabolites; transcriptomics of 24 peel samples; mGWAS in 154 pummelo accessions
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter for overexpression; FNS and PAL promoters used in reporter assays
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Only one medium-expression sweet orange transgenic seedling survived, highlighting dosage sensitivity. The study proposes promoter SVs as the evolutionary basis for reduced CmtMYB108 expression in pummelo and consequent flavone enhancement.
